title: pinus pallasiana subsp. caramanica
intro: Pinus pallasiana subsp. caramanica, also known as Korean stone pine, is a coniferous tree species native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The essential oils and resin extracted from the tree have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving respiratory function, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the pine needles and resin are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
